scope_note: QA Witt Tower det=-1 General v_p Period Law cert [440]. Twin of [439] for the det=-1 companion-matrix family. For M=[[t,1],[1,0]] (char poly x^2-tx-1, det=-1), pâ‰¡1 mod 4 prime, v_p(t^2+4)=r exactly (p^r|(t^2+4), p^(r+1) does not), c_r=(t^2+4)/p^r with gcd(c_r,p)=1, the orbit-count distribution on (Z/p^k Z)^2 is: STRUCTURAL CONSTANT: count(1)=1 for ALL k -- the only fixed point of M on (Z/p^k Z)^2 is the zero vector, because M*x=x forces t*a=0 mod p^k and since p not|t (as p|t^2+4 with p odd requires t^2â‰¡-4, hence tâ‰¡0 mod p would give 4â‰¡0 mod p impossible for p>2), this gives a=b=0. PERIOD SET: {1,4}âˆª{4*p^L:L=1..k} -- base cycle length is forced to 4 because the double eigenvalue mod p has order exactly 4 (Î»_0^2â‰¡-1 mod p, which is solvable iff pâ‰¡1 mod 4). count(4)=(p^min(r,k)-1)/4 -- period-4 orbit count, grows from (p-1)/4 at k=1 to (p^r-1)/4 at k=r, then saturates. [k<=r]: count(4*p^L)=(p-1)/4*p^(k-1) for L=1..k (k joint birth layers). [k>r]: count(4*p^L)=(p^2-1)/4*p^(L+r-2) for L=1..k-r (frozen); count(4*p^L)=(p-1)/4*p^(k-1) for L=k-r+1..k (r joint birth layers). ALGEBRAIC MECHANISM: K=t*I-2*M=[[-t,-2],[-2,t]]; det(K)=-(t^2+4)=-p^r*c_r (exact integer identity); M^4*x=x reduces to K*xâ‰¡0 mod p^k; therefore |ker(K mod p^k)|=p^min(r,k), giving count(4)=(p^min(r,k)-1)/4 period-4 orbits. COMPARISON WITH [439]: ALL non-trivial counts are divided by 4 relative to [439]: birth=(p-1)/4*p^(k-1) vs (p-1)*p^(k-1); frozen=(p^2-1)/4*p^(L+r-2) vs (p^2-1)*p^(L+r-2); count(period-4) has no det=+1 analogue (the det=+1 'count(1)' is p^min(r,k) while count(period-4) here is (p^min(r,k)-1)/4 -- shifted by 1 and divided by 4). NO pâ‰¡3 mod 4 EXCEPTION: for pâ‰¡3 mod 4, t^2+4â‰¡0 mod p has no solution (since -4 is a non-residue mod p when pâ‰¡3 mod 4), so no ramified primes exist in the det=-1 family for those p. This is structurally impossible, not a stall. (C1) DM1_PERIOD_SET: {1,4,4p^L:L=1..k} for p=5, r=1,2, k=1..4; zero violations PASS. (C2) DM1_COUNT4_SATURATION: count(4)=(p^min(r,k)-1)/4; p=5,13, r=1..3, k=1..4/2; zero failures PASS. (C3) DM1_UNIFIED_FORMULA: full law; p=5 r=1..3 k=1..4, p=13 r=1..3 k=1..2; zero violations PASS. (C4) DM1_FIXED_ALWAYS_1: count(1)=1 for all t with p|t^2+4, p=5,13, k=1..3; PASS. (C5) DM1_ALGEBRAIC_KER: ker(tI-2M mod p^k)=p^min(r,k), t=1..100, p=5,13, k=1..3; PASS. (C6) DM1_NO_RAMIFIED_P3MOD4: p=7,11,19,23 admit no t with p|t^2+4; PASS. 6 checks PASS; 7/7 fixtures PASS; self-test ok. Primary sources: Serre, J.-P. (1979) 'Local Fields' doi:10.1007/978-1-4757-5673-9 (ramification groups, p-adic fields); Ireland, K. and Rosen, M. (1990) 'A Classical Introduction to Modern Number Theory' ISBN 978-0-387-97329-6 Ch.5,7 (quadratic residues, Hensel lifting); Wall, D.D.
